quote.text: The best way to predict the future is to implement it
quote.author: David Heinemeier Hansson

links.title: CSS Variables with JavaScript
links.description: Did you know you can control CSS variables from JavaScript? This short article will take a very practical approach to show you how to do so by connecting a box positioned with CSS absolute positioning and mouse movements tracked in JavaScript.

links.title: Cache your React event listeners to improve performance.
links.description: An under-appreciated concept in JavaScript is how objects and functions are references, and that directly impacts React performance.

links.title: Next Generation Package Management
links.description: What if installs were so fast they could happen in the background, just by using Node? What if every file in your dependencies could be guaranteed to be bit-by-bit identical to what’s on the registry?

links.title: Inside look at modern web browser
links.description: Learn how browser turn your code into functional website from high-level architecture to the specifics of the rendering pipeline.

links.title: 10 years of Speed in Chrome
links.description: Speed has been one of Chrome’s four core principles, since it was first launched ten years ago.

links.title: Git Cheatsheet
links.description: Interactive Git Cheatsheet, categorizing commands based on what they affect.

links.title: Flexbox: How Big Is That Flexible Box?
links.description: This article explores the often confusing issue of sizing in Flexbox. How does Flexbox decide how big things should be?

book.title: ReactJS by Example- Building Modern Web Applications with React
book.author: Vipul A M
book.description: tarting with a project on Open Library API, you will be introduced to React and JSX before moving on to learning about the life cycle of a React component. In the second project, building a multi-step wizard form, you will learn about composite dynamic components and perform DOM actions. You will also learn about building a fast search engine by exploring server-side rendering in the third project on a search engine application. Next, you will build a simple frontpage for an e-commerce app in the fourth project by using data models and React add-ons. In the final project you will develop a complete social media tracker by using the flux way of defining React apps and know about the best practices and use cases with the help of ES6 and redux.
